name: thesun
description: Autonomous MCP server generation and orchestration platform. Generates, tests, and operates MCP servers with near-zero human involvement.
author: Tim Schwarz

commands.name: sun-status
commands.description: View thesun build status and system health

commands.name: sun-auth
commands.description: Capture authentication from any webapp. Opens browser, you log in, tokens saved. Use: /sun-auth <service> <login-url>

skills.name: optimize-mcp-creation
skills.description: Fully autonomous MCP creation pipeline. PROACTIVELY USE THIS when: (1) user wants to interact with an API/tool that has no existing MCP server, (2) user asks about a service/API integration that Claude cannot directly access, (3) user mentions wanting to automate a workflow with an external tool, (4) user says 'use thesun for X' or 'create an MCP for X'. Single invocation — researches API ecosystem, designs task-oriented tools, generates via thesun, scores quality, auto-fixes until passing. THE default for ALL MCP creation.

skills.name: generate-mcp
skills.description: Direct thesun generation without intelligence gathering or quality scoring. Use optimize-mcp-creation instead for all normal MCP creation. Only use this skill to bypass the optimizer for rapid iteration on known-working MCPs or debugging the builder itself.

skills.name: fix-mcp
skills.description: Fix, repair, or improve an existing MCP server. Use when: (1) user reports an MCP has build errors or test failures, (2) user wants to improve/update an existing MCP, (3) MCP is not starting correctly or has issues. Uses thesun FIX mode: thesun({ target: 'toolname', fix: '/path/to/mcp' }). Validates, identifies issues, and autonomously fixes problems.

skills.name: validate-mcp
skills.description: Validate an MCP server and return a quality score (0-100) WITHOUT modifying it. Use for auditing, pre-deployment checks, or quality comparison. Checks: package.json, entry point, README, tests, build, graceful startup.

skills.name: list-mcps
skills.description: List all MCP servers registered in Claude configuration. Shows installed MCPs, their paths, and status. Use when user asks 'what MCPs do I have?' or 'is X MCP installed?'

skills.name: build-status
skills.description: Check status of active MCP builds and view system health dashboard. Use when user asks about build progress, costs, or system health.

skills.name: research-api
skills.description: Research and discover all APIs for a tool WITHOUT generating code. Use for exploration and planning before committing to full MCP generation. Finds docs, OpenAPI specs, existing MCPs, and gaps.

skills.name: update-mcps
skills.description: Proactively search the web to improve and update MCP servers. Finds new API features, security vulnerabilities, and best practices from other implementations. Works on both local and non-local MCPs. Documents changes on Confluence and GitHub. Use for maintenance, security audits, or keeping MCPs current.

agents.name: mcp-optimizer
agents.description: Intelligence layer for MCP generation. Pre-generation API analysis, reference template selection, accumulated knowledge consultation, blueprint generation, and post-generation 100-point quality assessment.

agents.name: meta-planner
agents.description: High-level strategic planner (Opus) that adapts workflows dynamically. Analyzes requests, anticipates problems, and designs custom approaches when standard workflow doesn't fit. Should be invoked FIRST for any complex request.

agents.name: mcp-builder
agents.description: Autonomous agent that generates complete MCP servers from tool specifications. Runs discovery, generation, testing, security scanning, and optimization with minimal human intervention.

agents.name: api-researcher
agents.description: Researches and catalogs all APIs for a tool. Finds existing MCPs, vendor docs, OpenAPI specs, and identifies gaps.

agents.name: mcp-updater
agents.description: Autonomous agent that searches the web for MCP improvements and applies updates. Monitors API changelogs, security advisories, and community best practices. Updates both local and non-local MCPs. Documents changes on Confluence and GitHub.
